Facts.text: Battle of Callinicum: Roman general Belisarius defeated by Sasanian army led by Azarethes, though heavy losses on both sides result in a stalemate in northern Syria
Comet 1P/607 H1 (Halley) approaches within 0.0898 AUs of Earth
Anglo-Saxon Archbishop of Canterbury Alphege murdered by Vikings while being held hostage by them in Greenwich, London
Alam Shah of Delhi resigns throne
Charles, protestant German monarch, signs Treaty of Frankrfurt
Theatrum Anatomicum opens in Amsterdam
Adriana Nooseman becomes  Dutch 1st actress to perform on stage professionally in the Netherlands in the play "Onvergelijkelijke Ariana" at Amsterdam Schouwburg
Amsterdam buys Van Aerssens family 1/3 part of Suriname
Minutemen Capt John Parker orders not to fire unless fired upon
New England militiamen begin the siege of Boston, hemming in the British army garrison
Venezuela achieves home rule: Vicente Emparan, Governor of the Captaincy General is removed by the people of Caracas and a Junta is installed.
33 patriotic exiles return to Uruguay
Nikolai Gogol's "Revisor" premieres in St Petersburg
Cheyney University forms as the Institute for Colored Youth
Treaty of London constitutes Belgium an independent kingdom & Luxembourg a Grand Duchy
California Historical Society is founded
Netherlands Van Hall government forms
Baltimore riots - 4 soldiers, 9 civilians killed
Lincoln orders blockade of Confederate ports (Civil War)
Union troops and fleet occupy For Huger, Virginia
Barracks on Alcatraz Island destroyed in fire
Opera "Les Cloches de Cornerville" is produced (Paris)

British banker Thomas Agnew and others found the Liverpool Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (LSPCC), based on Agnew's observation of a similar group in NYC; its success leads to a national organization
Charles Duryea takes the 1st American-made automotive for a test drive
1st Boston Marathon (B.A.A. Road Race), won by John J. McDermott in 2:55:10; the world's oldest annual marathon inspired by success of the first marathon at the 1896 Summer Olympics
Highest scoring opening game, Phils beat Braves 19-17 in 10
Much of Toronto destroyed by fire
Belgian naval education ship Comte The Stain de Naeyer sets sail
SF Earthquake ends killing 452
A convention with Turkey recognizes Bulgarian independence
"Bing Boys are Here" opens in London

Alderman Kelly reads the 'Castle Order' to a meeting of Dublin Corporation; this forged document supposedly from Dublin Castle, indicated that there was to be mass arrests of Irish Volunteers to prevent "trouble"
Italians troops conquer Col di Lana at Merano
André Messager's romantic opera "Monsieur Beaucaire", based on Booth Tarkington's novel, opens at the Prince's Theatre in London, England
French assembly decides on 8 hour work day
Funeral of last German Emperoress, Augusta Victoria
New Egyptian law allows suffrage for men, except soldiers
"National Barn Dance" premieres on WLS Chicago
"Vagabond King" opens in London
Japanese troops occupies Sjantung-schiereiland
New York Yankees are out of 1st place for 1st time since May 1926
The 125th and final fascicle of the Oxford English Dictionary is published
First day of the Great Uprising in Palestine, anti-Jewish riots break out
Baker Bowl, Philadelphia: Phillies infielder Emmett "Heine" Mueller & Brooklyn Dodger outfielder Ernie Koy both homer in their 1st MLB at bats
Connecticut finally approves Bill of Rights (148 years late)
"Lake Shore Ltd" derails speed killing 34 near Little Falls, New York
Dutch prime minister De Geer declares state of siege
Bulgarian troops invade Macedonia
Milk rationed in Holland
1944 NFL Draft: Angelo Bertelli from University of Notre Dame first pick by Boston Yanks
Allied fleet attack Sabang Sumatra
Diplomatic relations between the Soviet Union and Guatemala are established
US aircraft carrier Franklin heavy damaged in Japanese air raid
US offensive against Shuri-barrier on Okinawa
Yankees switch from 3rd base to 1st base dug out
AAU record for a 25-foot rope climb is set in 4.7 seconds
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, Ontario: Toronto Maple Leafs edge Montreal Canadiens, 2-1 for a 4-2 series win; first all-Canadian final since 1935
Suh Yun Buck wins world record marathon (2:25:39)
American Broadcasting Company (ABC) TV network debuts
WAFB TV channel 9 in Baton Rouge, LA (CBS) begins broadcasting
The German automaker Volkswagen, after six years of selling cars in the United States, founds Volkswagen of America in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey to standardize its dealer and service network
1st MLB baseball game in New Jersey - Brooklyn Dodgers beat Philadelphia Phillies, 5-4, at Roosevelt Stadium in Jersey City
Uprising in La Paz Bolivia, fails
Baseball uniforms begin displaying players' names on their backs
Comiskey Park's famed "exploding" scoreboard begins operating
Rightist coup in Laos, Suvanna Phuma remains premier
1st all news radio station (WINS 1010 AM in NYC) begins operating
At a cost of $20,000, the outer Astrodome ceiling is painted because of sun's glare, this causes the grass to die
T.A.M.I. Show premieres in London
In 1st regular season game at Anaheim Stadium, Angels lose 3-1 to Chic
The Beatles sign a contract agreeing to stay together for 10 years (they don't)
Yugoslav author Mihaljo Mihaljov sentenced 4½ years
Belgian construction workers strike
NL owners approve expansion for 2 new teams
Serious rioting in the Bogside area of Derry following clashes between Northern Ireland Civil Rights Association marchers and Loyalists and members of the Royal Ulster Constabulary
24th Tony Awards: "Borstal Boy" (play) & "Applause" (musical) win
Sierra Leone becomes a republic (National Day)
USSR Salyut 1 launched; 1st manned lab in orbit
Bangladesh becomes a member of the British Commonwealth
Hungary revises its constitution to declare itself a socialist state
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Oriole Al Bumbry hits an inside-the-park HR against NY Yankees
British rock band Queen begins concert tour of Japan
India launches 1st satellite with help of USSR
Yitzhak Navron elected 5th president of Israel
FCC raids & shuts down pirate radio station WFAT (Brooklyn New York)
Oakland A's runs record to 11-0
Former Cuban marathon cheat Rosie Ruiz is arrested for embezzling $60,000
Guinon Bluford announced as 1st African American NASA astronaut
USSR Salyut 7 space station put into orbit
France performs nuclear test
Advance Australia Fair is proclaimed as Australia's national anthem, with green and gold the national colors
Nemesis, death star of dinosaurs 1st appears in print (Nature magazine)
16th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-D): Discovery 4 returns to Earth
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Brendon Kuruppu scores 201* on Test Cricket debut (Sri Lanka v NZ)
Brewers score 5 runs in 9th to win 6-4 & record 12th straight AL win
Jacqueline Blanc, sets women's downhill ski speed rec (124.902 mph)
LA Clippers end season with a terrible 12-70 record
Last wild condor captured on California wildlife reserve
USSR performs underground nuclear test

Central Park Five: Violent rape of jogger Trisha Meili in NYC's Central Park becomes one of the most widely publicized crimes of the 1980s. Five teenagers are wrongfully convicted and spend between 6-12 years in prison.
Gun turret explodes on USS Iowa, killing 47 sailors
Kevin Elster (NY Mets), sets error-less shortstop mark at 73
Republic Day in Sierra Leone
Contra guerrillas, leftist Sandinistas & incoming government agree to truce n Nicaragua's civil war
Pistons & 76'ers get into a fight accruing $162,500 fines (NBA record)
Greyhound Bus posts $195 million loss for 1990
After a 51 day siege by the FBI 76 Branch Davidians die in a fire near Waco Texas (accident, suicide, tear gas are disputed causes)
Fire in psychiatric institute in South Korea, kills 40
South Dakota governor George S. Mickelson and seven others are killed when a state-owned aircraft crash lands in Iowa
"Illmatic" debut studio album by US rapper Nas is released
Graeme Obree bicycles world record 10km (11:25.88)
Inkatha ends boycott of South African multi-racial election
US Supreme Court outlaws excluding people from juries because of gender
Chopper 4 1st used on WNBC TV (NYC) news
Rangers scores 16 in 8th vs Orioles
South Africa defeat Pakistan to win the Pepsi Cup in Sharjah
NFL Draft: Ohio State offensive tackle Orlando Pace first pick by St. Louis Rams
Renee Slaughter crowned 14th Miss Hawaiian Tropic Intl
San Diego Padres & St Louis Cards play at Aloha Stadium Hawaii
German Reichstag reopens with new glass dome by architect Norman Foster
The German Bundestag returns to Berlin.
The Space Needle, built in 1961, is declared a local historic landmark by city council of Seattle, Washington
Boston bombing suspects killed and captured in Boston after 4 days
Boat carrying approx. 850 migrants is shipwrecked in the Mediterranean between Italian and Libya, with only 27 migrants rescued.
Fox News confirms they would be letting go of Bill O'Reilly after allegations of sexual harassment
King Mswati III of Swaziland changes the name of Swaziland to Eswatini, which translates as the “Land of the Swati”
US COVID-19 death toll passes 40,000, with 742,442 cases recorded according to Johns Hopkins tally
Cuba's Communist party announces Miguel Díaz-Canel will replaceRaúl Castroas party leader, after the latter steps down
In Las Vegas, Nevada: San Jose Sharks forward Patrick Marleau breaks Gordie Howe's NHL record for most games played, now at 1,768 and counting
NASA successfully flies its drone helicopter Ingenuity on Mars, first powered aircraft to fly on another world
New Zealand and Australia open a travel bubble between the two countries after more than a year of border closures
US inflation hits 8.5%, the highest since 1981, driven by a rise in gas prices, rent and food
At least 78 people are killed, crushed in a crowd waiting for handouts at a school in Sanaa, Yemen during a Ramadan event
World's largest-ever election begins in India with 968 million eligible voters, in the first of seven stages ending 1 June
PopeClement VIIfires Dutch Inquisitor-General Frans Van der Hulst
The French city of Chartres surrenders to KingHenry IV
Holy Roman EmperorCharles VIissues the Pragmatic Sanction, decreeing that Habsburg possessions could be inherited by a daughter

British explorer CaptainJames Cookfirst sights Australia. Writes in his log book that “what we have as yet seen of this land appears rather low, and not very hilly, the face of the Country green and Woody, but the Sea shore is all a white Sand.”
Christoph Willibald Gluck's opera "Iphigénie en Aulide", based on the play "Iphigenia in Aulis" byEuripidespremieres in Paris
Paul Revere, William Dawes and Samuel Prescott are captured by British troops riding from Lexington to Concord, Prescott escapes to warn Concord as does Dawes who acts as a decoy
John Adamssecures Dutch Republic's recognition of the United States as an independent government, a house he purchased in The Hague becomes America's first embassy
Henry Morton Stanleyis awarded the Order of Leopold in Brussels, proclaimed a hero and taken from one banquet hall to another
Jules Massenet's opera "Werther" premieres in NYC

In the Philippines, recently captured insurgent leaderEmilio Aguinaldoissues a proclamation advising his countrymen to end their rebellion and use of peaceful means to work with the US toward independence.
George Bernard Shaw's play "Fanny's First Play" premieres in London
Bonnie Parkeris captured in a failed hardware store burglary, and subsequently jailed; a grand jury fails to indict her, however, and she is released a few months later
US PresidentHerbert Hooversuggests five-day work week
FDRannounces US will leave gold standard
Shirley Templeappears in her 1st feature length film, "Stand Up & Cheer"
"Bride of Frankenstein" horror film classic and sequel to "Frankenstein" starringBoris Karloffand Elsa Lancaster is released
Bertolt Brecht's play "Mother Courage and her Children" premieres in Zurich
Bicycle Day - Swiss chemist Dr.Albert Hofmanndeliberately takes LSD for the first time
Jews refuse to surrender the Warsaw Ghetto to SS officer Jürgen Stroop, who then orders its destruction, beginning the Warsaw Ghetto Uprising
After scoring 45 goals during the regular season, Montreal Canadiens'Maurice Richardis named MVP
Chiang Kai-shekelected President of Nationalist China
Yankees dedicate a plaque forBabe Ruth
GeneralDouglas MacArthurends his military career
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 46,900m
Johnny Cashreleases single "Ring of Fire" written by his future wifeJune Carterand Merle Kilgore
Roger Sessions' opera "Montezuma" premieres in West Berlin, Germany
"Whipped Cream & Other Delights", fourth full album byHerb Alpert& the Tijuana Brass Band, is released
"Casino Royale" James Bond comedy film starringDavid NivenandPeter Sellerspremieres. Based on the Ian Fleming novel.
Charles Mansonsentenced to life in prison for the murder ofSharon Tate
British Prime MinisterEdward Heathconfirms that a plan to conduct an arrest operation, in the event of a riot during the march on 30 January 1972, was known to British government Ministers in advance
Barbra Streisandrecords "Between Yesterday & Tomorrow"
Sally Rideis named the 1st American woman astronaut
Michael Spinks beatsLarry Holmesin 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Marla Maplesappears on ABC's Prime-Time
Evander Holyfieldbeats George Foreman in 12 for heavyweight boxing title
Oklahoma City bombing:Timothy McVeighsets a truck bomb at Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building, killing 168. including 19 children, and injuring 500
Nina Simoneis awarded an honorary degree by the Curtis Institute of Music in Philadelphia
Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger elected PopeBenedict XVIon the second day of the Papal conclave.
Fidel Castroresigns his position of First Secretary of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of Cuba after 45 years in power
Miguel Diaz-Canel is elected Cuba's new president, after former presidentRaúl Castrosteps down
US SenatorTammy Duckworth(Illinois-D) is the first parent to bring a baby into the Senate chambers, a day after the Senate votes to allow babies on the chamber's floor
UK COVID-19 death toll reaches 16,060 (hospitals only), as "The Sunday Times" criticizesBoris Johnson's government's response, saying they "sleepwalked into disaster"
USBidenadministration restores climate impacts and community say to the National Environmental Policy Act, previously removed byDonald Trump
Taylor Swiftreleases her 11th studio album "The Tortured Poets Department" setting a Spotify record for over 300 million streams in one day
American Revolution begins in Lexington, Massachusetts, with the "Shot Heard Round the World" fired later that day in Concord

ActressMae Westfound guilty of “obscenity and corrupting the morals of youth” in a New York stage play entitled "Sex". She is sentenced to 10 days in prison and fined $500, the resulting publicity launches her Hollywood career.
Rodney Kingawarded $3,800,000 compensation by the Los Angeles County for his police beating
Turkey passes Iran to become the Middle Eastern country with the most COVID-19 cases with 86,306 infections, Iran continues to have the most deaths

Ruby Johnson, American soul singer ("I'll Run Your Hurt Away"), born in Elizabeth City, North Carolina (d. 1999)

Elinor Donahue, American actress (Father Knows Best, Get a Life), born in Tacoma, Washington
Derrick Grant, Scottish rugby union flanker (14 Tests; Hawick RFC) and coach (Scotland 1985-88), born in Hawick, Scotland (d. 2024)

Jonathan Tunick, American orchestrator, musical director, and composer (Soundheim musicals), one of 19 'EGOT' winners - having won all four major American show business awards: Emmy; Grammy, Oscar; and Tony, born in New York City
Clay Shaw, American politician (Rep-R-FL, 1981-2007), born in Miami, Florida (d. 2013)
Ellen Weston, American actress (Betty-SWAT), born in New York City

Bas Jan Ader, Dutch artist and photographer, born in Winschoten, Netherlands (disappeared in 1975).
